verses.text: The Servant says the LORD called him from the womb, made his mouth a sharp sword, and declared, 'Thou art my servant... in whom I will be glorified.' This is Christ. The Father prepared and prized His Servant from the first.

The Servant says the LORD called him from the womb, made his mouth a sharp sword, and declared, 'Thou art my servant... in whom I will be glorified.' This is Christ. The Father prepared and prized His Servant from the first.

The Servant says the LORD called him from the womb, made his mouth a sharp sword, and declared, 'Thou art my servant... in whom I will be glorified.' This is Christ. The Father prepared and prized His Servant from the first.

Though his labour seemed in vain, his reward is with God, who makes him not only to restore Israel but 'a light to the Gentiles... my salvation unto the end of the earth.' His mission is worldwide. Christ brings salvation to all nations.

Though his labour seemed in vain, his reward is with God, who makes him not only to restore Israel but 'a light to the Gentiles... my salvation unto the end of the earth.' His mission is worldwide. Christ brings salvation to all nations.

Though his labour seemed in vain, his reward is with God, who makes him not only to restore Israel but 'a light to the Gentiles... my salvation unto the end of the earth.' His mission is worldwide. Christ brings salvation to all nations.

The LORD speaks to him whom man despises; kings shall rise and worship, for God gives him 'for a covenant of the people,' to free prisoners. The rejected One is exalted. Christ, scorned by men, is honoured by God and frees the captive.

The LORD speaks to him whom man despises; kings shall rise and worship, for God gives him 'for a covenant of the people,' to free prisoners. The rejected One is exalted. Christ, scorned by men, is honoured by God and frees the captive.

The LORD speaks to him whom man despises; kings shall rise and worship, for God gives him 'for a covenant of the people,' to free prisoners. The rejected One is exalted. Christ, scorned by men, is honoured by God and frees the captive.

His people shall not hunger or thirst, for 'he that hath mercy on them shall lead them,' and they shall come from far. He shepherds them home. The Servant gathers the scattered with tender, faithful care.

His people shall not hunger or thirst, for 'he that hath mercy on them shall lead them,' and they shall come from far. He shepherds them home. The Servant gathers the scattered with tender, faithful care.

His people shall not hunger or thirst, for 'he that hath mercy on them shall lead them,' and they shall come from far. He shepherds them home. The Servant gathers the scattered with tender, faithful care.

Though Zion says, 'The LORD hath forsaken me,' He answers, 'Can a woman forget her sucking child?... yet will I not forget thee. Behold, I have graven thee upon the palms of my hands.' His love is unfailing. God's people are engraved on His hands, never out of His mind.

Though Zion says, 'The LORD hath forsaken me,' He answers, 'Can a woman forget her sucking child?... yet will I not forget thee. Behold, I have graven thee upon the palms of my hands.' His love is unfailing. God's people are engraved on His hands, never out of His mind.

Though Zion says, 'The LORD hath forsaken me,' He answers, 'Can a woman forget her sucking child?... yet will I not forget thee. Behold, I have graven thee upon the palms of my hands.' His love is unfailing. God's people are engraved on His hands, never out of His mind.
Her children hasten back, and she shall wear them like jewels, marvelling, 'who hath begotten me these?' Desolation turns to fullness. God multiplies His people far beyond their hope.

Her children hasten back, and she shall wear them like jewels, marvelling, 'who hath begotten me these?' Desolation turns to fullness. God multiplies His people far beyond their hope.

God lifts His hand to the Gentiles, and kings shall be 'nursing fathers' to His people; 'they shall not be ashamed that wait for me.' The nations serve Zion. Those who wait on God will never be disappointed.

God lifts His hand to the Gentiles, and kings shall be 'nursing fathers' to His people; 'they shall not be ashamed that wait for me.' The nations serve Zion. Those who wait on God will never be disappointed.

Shall the prey be taken from the mighty? Yes; God will contend with their captors, 'and all flesh shall know that I the LORD am thy Saviour.' He rescues the helpless. The strong cannot hold what God determines to set free.
